translated: The cheers were so loud, it felt like everyone had been waiting for "UNIVERSE!!"
How long has it been since we last played it?
I was so moved by everyone singing along during the finale.
I even took off my in-ears to listen.
We extended the length of the "woah-woah" part at the beginning this time.
Hearing the voices get louder and louder was such a blast.
We got to sing on the sub-stage too.
How was it for the 2nd and 3rd gen members?

It was so fun! We were so close that I could see everyone's faces clearly. Some people were having a blast shouting "woah-woah," while others were sobbing as they sang. Seeing that almost made me tear up too.
Moments like that remind me how amazing it is that so many people gather to share the same moment and give so much energy to Liella.
I'm so grateful we could get closer to everyone. It really means a lot.
I feel like as we grow up, we have fewer chances to cry or laugh with our whole hearts
compared to when we were kids. So seeing them show us those precious expressions...
it makes me so happy to think they trust us enough to let out all their emotions like that. That's so true.
I want to do it again.
The 7th Live really felt like we were creating the songs together with the audience more than ever before.
There were so many. So many.
We had so much fun too. We've seen so many people saying they had a great time,
but we really had a blast ourselves too.
On Day 1 of the Aichi performance, people probably expected "Mirai no Oto ga Kikoeru" next.
Especially those who had been to the Kanagawa shows
They probably thought "Mirai no Oto ga Kikoeru" was coming up,
but then I asked the crowd if they could shout "woah-woah" again for the next song.
That was during Day 1 of the Aichi performance.
Then, everyone mistakenly thought "Open the Gate" was coming...
Well, it wasn't exactly a mistake.
A lot of people thought we were doing "Open the Gate" for a third time, so they were all ready to shout, but then "UNIVERSE!!" started.
Doing the same song three times would've been quite a memory,
A "woah-woah" song came out of nowhere, which was a surprise.
But "Open the Gate" also has a lot of call-and-response,
I'm glad we had so many songs like that this time.
I hope we can perform them again. I really do.
Thank you! And thank you all for sending in so many emails.
Now, let's introduce a mail for the "Lunch Time Request" segment.
This one is from "Rieraji Name" Tetsuo-san.
